title: Koanophyllon littorale
intro: Koanophyllon littorale, also known as Littoral Leaf, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon littorale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Koanophyllon littorale is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to treat various skin conditions and infections.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon littorale belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a flowering plant native to the Americas. The plant is classified as a species within the genus Koanophyllon. Koanophyllon littorale is often found growing in a variety of environments, including coastal areas and dry woodlands. Further classification of this plant is within the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida.
